skill.name: azure-ai-voicelive-dotnet
skill.description: Guidance for building Azure AI Voice Live applications with the .NET SDK, including setup, authentication, sessions, events, voices, and audio configuration.
skill.summary: Build real-time Azure AI Voice Live assistants with .NET.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, network, environment, cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown documentation examples. No prompt injection, hidden execution, malicious network behavior, or credential exfiltration was found. The only residual issue is low-risk credential-handling documentation that includes an API key placeholder and a hardcoded-key example.

low_findings.title: API Key Placeholder and Hardcoded-Key Example
low_findings.description: The documentation shows an API key environment variable and a hardcoded API key example. This is not exfiltration, but it can encourage unsafe credential handling if copied without changes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key values are placeholders, so there is no exposed secret. The pattern is still a real documentation risk because users may copy hardcoded credential examples.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: Package installation commands appear inside fenced Markdown examples. The skill contains no executable script or automation that would run these commands on behalf of the user.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are inside Markdown code fences and are ordinary .NET package installation instructions. No wrapper script, hook, or runtime execution path is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Official Documentation URLs Flagged as Network Access
low_findings.description: Azure endpoint placeholders and reference links were flagged as network behavior. They are documentation references to Azure, Microsoft Learn, NuGet, and GitHub, not automatic outbound requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation values and official service references. There is no hidden destination, data collection endpoint, or code path that sends user data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Signals Dismissed
low_findings.description: The scanner matched benign words and API names as cryptography or reconnaissance indicators. The referenced lines describe function parameters, model names, voice detection settings, or best practices.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context contains no cryptographic function calls and no system enumeration command. The detections appear caused by generic token matches in documentation text.

content.value_statement: Real-time voice applications require careful session, audio, and authentication setup. This skill gives concise .NET guidance for Azure AI Voice Live clients, events, voices, and function calling.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to install Azure.AI.VoiceLive and related .NET packages.
Shows supported authentication patterns for Entra ID and API keys.
Describes VoiceLiveClient and VoiceLiveSession workflows for real-time sessions.
Maps key session update event types for audio, text, errors, and function calls.
Summarizes voice choices, realtime model options, and PCM audio settings.
Provides best practices for modalities, voice activity detection, disposal, and error handling.
limitations: It is a reference guide, not a complete runnable application.
It does not manage Azure resources, roles, billing, or deployment for you.
Version details may need verification against current Azure SDK releases.
Audio capture and playback require separate application code and device handling.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Voice Assistant
use_cases.description: Use the session workflow and event handling notes to build an initial C# voice assistant quickly.

use_cases.title: Plan Azure Voice Integration
use_cases.description: Review authentication, model, voice, and audio format choices before adding realtime voice to a product.

use_cases.title: Debug Session Behavior
use_cases.description: Use the event type reference and error handling guidance to inspect response, audio, and function call flow.

prompt_templates.title: Install the SDK
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add Azure AI Voice Live to a new .NET project and explain the required packages.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Voice Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the steps to create and configure an Azure AI Voice Live session in C# using Entra ID.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Realtime Events
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design an event loop for Azure AI Voice Live that handles audio, text, errors, and completion events.

prompt_templates.title: Add Function Calling
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add function calling to an Azure AI Voice Live assistant and safely return tool results to the session.

output_examples.input: I need to start a realtime Azure voice assistant in C#.
output_examples.output: The skill would outline package setup, endpoint configuration, Entra ID authentication, session creation, and basic modality settings.

output_examples.input: My assistant receives audio but does not return expected text.
output_examples.output: The skill would point you to session update handling, response completion events, and error message checks.

output_examples.input: I want the voice assistant to call a weather tool.
output_examples.output: The skill would describe function definition, argument handling, result submission, and response restart flow.

best_practices: Prefer DefaultAzureCredential and managed identity over hardcoded API keys.
Handle every relevant session update type, including errors and completion events.
Configure text and audio modalities together for natural voice assistant behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not paste production API keys into source code or examples.
Do not ignore session errors during realtime audio processing.
Do not assume audio devices, sample rates, or channels are already compatible.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you design .NET applications that use Azure AI Voice Live for realtime voice interaction.

faq.question: Does it include a complete sample app?
faq.answer: No. It provides setup, workflow, and reference guidance that you can apply in your own project.

faq.question: Which authentication method is recommended?
faq.answer: The guidance recommends Microsoft Entra ID through DefaultAzureCredential when possible.

faq.question: Can it help with function calling?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how a session can define a tool, receive arguments, and return a tool result.

faq.question: Does it handle microphone capture?
faq.answer: No. It mentions audio libraries, but device capture and playback code remain application responsibilities.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found documentation examples only, with a low warning about credential-handling examples.
